requirement: Plan a focused follow-up implementation pass for the confirmed post-T1 misses and partial fixes only on the existing light-mode workstream. Scope candidates from the current audit: 1) light-mode --surface-card-top is still pure white, 2) circle of fifths slice borders remain uneven or visually incomplete, 3) stepper, shared toggle, and nav hover states are still too subtle, 4) degree-chip-strip light-mode degree-color labels are still black, 5) degree-chip-strip and fretboard degree-color contrast remain too indistinct, 6) light-mode fret wire still reads too blue, 7) light-mode shared accent colors remain too similar.

r1.repo_shape: React 19 + TypeScript + Vite app. Theme/state styling is centralized through src/styles/{tokens,semantic,themes,App}.css, then composed through CSS Modules in src/components/**, with fretboard materials split between CSS tokens and SVG defs/background rendering. HEAD is commit 4d0ba6c on light-mode-warm-pass/feat-light-mode-palette.
local_instructions: Use conventional commits and conventional PR titles.
R1 stays read-only in the original checkout.
The repo lint path uses eslint plus stylelint via npm run lint.
Shared CSS refactors may affect selector-based tests and visual baselines.
likely_write_scopes: Shared surface/accent token scope: src/styles/themes.css, src/styles/semantic.css, src/styles/tokens.css, src/styles/App.css
Contract and snapshot scope: e2e/theme-contract.spec.ts and affected Playwright visual snapshots
conflict_risks: --surface-card-top fans into cards, strips, overlays, and panels through semantic aliases, so changing it can legitimately move multiple non-fretboard surfaces at once.
shared.module.css is a high-coupling file because the toggle, note-grid, accidental-toggle, and other control surfaces compose from it.
Circle-of-fifths slice visibility depends on both CSS stroke rules and SVG draw order, so a CSS-only border-width change may not fully solve the visual gap.
Degree-color contrast spans both chip and fretboard paths and is split between shared accent tokens, local degree-color selectors, and core degree color data.
Fret wire appearance is split between light-mode theme tokens and SVG gradient/shadow rendering, so changing only the token values or only the defs can leave inconsistent output.
Retuning the shared light-mode accent family will fan out into branding, control chrome, circle states, note rings, and any contract tests that still assert the current warm palette.
worktree_signals: The current checkout is already on the existing light-mode feature branch and has only untracked planning docs, not overlapping code edits.
The narrowed follow-up scope still overlaps heavily in shared token files, shared chrome CSS, and fretboard/circle visual baselines.
Parallelism is limited because several confirmed misses depend on the same accent and surface tokens.
No clear isolated code-only or test-only branch exists yet; sequential execution remains the safer default unless the user explicitly wants a separate follow-up workstream.
unknowns: Whether this follow-up should continue on the existing light-mode branch/PR or be planned as a separate follow-up branch/PR.
Whether the surface-card-top correction should intentionally propagate to every surface that inherits that token, or be localized to only the most problematic light-mode surfaces.
Whether the accent-separation fix should retune the shared light-mode accent family globally, or stay limited to degree-color and fretboard-facing surfaces to reduce broader UI churn.

assumptions: This follow-up is limited to the confirmed post-T1 misses and partial fixes from the current audit, not a re-scope of the original 16-item implementation pass.
No layout or structural redesign is intended; the work remains token, CSS-module, SVG-def, and targeted selector refinement only.
Dark-mode behavior should change only where the confirmed miss explicitly applies in both themes, such as shared hover/focus chrome.
Theme-contract and light-mode visual snapshot updates are expected because the remaining issues still touch shared surface and accent tokens.

answers.accent_retune_scope: Retune the shared light-mode accent family

questions.question: Should this follow-up be planned as a continuation of the existing light-mode workstream, or as a separate follow-up workstream?
questions.why: R1 found the checkout is already on light-mode-warm-pass/feat-light-mode-palette at HEAD 4d0ba6c, and the remaining misses still overlap the same theme, fretboard, and circle files touched by T1.

options.label: Continue the existing workstream
options.description: Keep the remaining fixes on the current branch and PR path so overlapping token and fretboard files stay in one sequence.

options.label: Create a separate follow-up workstream
options.description: Plan a new branch and PR for the remaining fixes, accepting extra merge coordination to isolate the second pass.

questions.question: When correcting light-mode --surface-card-top, should the follow-up intentionally propagate that token change to all surfaces that inherit it, or narrow the fix to only the visibly problematic card-like surfaces?
questions.why: R1 confirmed --surface-card-top currently fans out through cards, strips, overlays, and panels via semantic aliases, so a token change can either create cohesive light-mode elevation or trigger broader-than-expected snapshot churn.

options.label: Propagate through the shared token
options.description: Retune the shared light-mode card-top token and let all inheriting surfaces move together for a consistent surface ladder.

options.label: Localize the surface fix
options.description: Keep the global token mostly stable and patch only the specific light-mode surfaces that currently read too white, reducing fan-out at the cost of more exceptions.

questions.question: Should the accent-separation follow-up retune the shared light-mode accent family globally, or stay mostly localized to degree-color and fretboard-facing surfaces?
questions.why: R1 confirmed the remaining contrast issues span both global accent tokens and local degree-color selectors, and the same amber/crimson family now drives branding, control chrome, circle states, note rings, and degree-color surfaces.

options.label: Retune the shared light-mode accent family
options.description: Widen the contrast at the token level so controls, circle slices, degree chips, and fretboard note roles all inherit clearer separation.

options.label: Localize accent changes to degree and fretboard surfaces
options.description: Keep the broader light-mode accent family mostly stable and fix the remaining contrast only where it is most visible, reducing broader UI churn.

acceptance_criteria: Fix only the confirmed post-T1 misses and partial fixes from the current audit.
Retune light-mode card-top surfaces away from pure white through the shared token path unless doing so creates a new explicit exception.
Improve shared hover visibility for the remaining weak stepper, toggle, and nav chrome without redesigning component structure.
Make circle-of-fifths slice borders visually complete and more uniform in light mode, including active/root slice visibility.
Correct degree-chip-strip degree-color labels in light mode and improve degree-color separation across both chip and fretboard surfaces.
Retune light-mode fret wire hue away from the current blue cast while keeping the existing material model coherent.
Widen the shared light-mode accent separation enough to improve note-ring, chip, and control-state differentiation.
Update any affected theme-contract expectations and intentional visual baselines.

execution_strategy.shape: single sequential chunk on the existing light-mode workstream
execution_strategy.rationale: The remaining misses overlap the same shared light-mode token files, shared control chrome, fretboard rendering, and light-mode snapshots, so splitting them would create overlapping write scopes and churn without clear independence.

chunk_graph.name: Post-T1 light-mode follow-up refinements
chunk_graph.ownership_boundary: Shared light-mode tokens, shared chrome, circle-of-fifths borders, degree-color contrast, fret wire/material tuning, and resulting test/snapshot updates

chunks.name: Resolve remaining light-mode follow-up gaps
chunks.goal: Implement the confirmed post-T1 misses and partial fixes on the existing light-mode workstream: non-white light-mode card-top surfaces, clearer shared hover chrome, more reliable circle-of-fifths slice borders, corrected degree-chip-strip degree-color labels, stronger degree-color contrast between chip and fretboard surfaces, warmer light-mode fret wire, and wider shared light-mode accent separation, including any required contract-test and visual-snapshot updates.
context: Continue the existing light-mode workstream on branch light-mode-warm-pass/feat-light-mode-palette instead of creating a separate follow-up branch.
Propagate the light-mode --surface-card-top correction through the shared token path rather than patching only local surfaces.
Retune the shared light-mode accent family globally enough to improve control, circle, chip, and fretboard differentiation.
This follow-up is limited to the confirmed post-T1 misses and partial fixes from the audit, not a reimplementation of the original 16-item pass.
Dark mode should change only where the remaining miss explicitly applies in both themes, such as shared hover or focus chrome.
implementation: Retune the shared light-mode card-top and dependent surface tokens so top-level card-like surfaces stop reading as pure white while keeping the surface ladder coherent.
Strengthen the remaining weak hover states across shared toggle, stepper, and nav-style controls so they are visually distinct from their background surfaces.
Fix circle-of-fifths slice border consistency and active/root edge visibility, including any SVG draw-order or selector adjustments needed beyond a simple stroke-width bump.
Correct light-mode degree-chip-strip degree-color label color and improve degree-color differentiation across both chip and fretboard surfaces.
Warm the light-mode fret wire hue away from the current blue-gray cast while keeping the existing fret-wire material rendering coherent with its shadow and highlight passes.
Retune the shared light-mode accent family enough to create clearer separation between note-ring, chord, scale, and control-accent states without redesigning the theme.
Update the affected contract assertions and refresh the intended light-mode visual snapshots.
chunks.commit: fix: resolve remaining light mode contrast and chrome gaps
chunks.pr_title: fix: resolve remaining light mode contrast and chrome gaps
chunks.pr_body:
Summary:
- finish the remaining light-mode card, accent, and hover refinements left after the first follow-up pass
- improve circle border consistency, degree-color readability, and fret wire warmth
- update affected contract expectations and light-mode visual baselines

Verification:
- npm run lint
- npm run test
- npm run test:e2e -- e2e/theme-contract.spec.ts
- npm run test:visual

Risks:
- shared token changes still fan out across cards, strips, controls, and note states
- circle border visibility depends on both CSS stroke rules and SVG draw order
- light-mode snapshot churn is expected across fretboard and circle surfaces
